title: PHP Conditions
content:
### PHP Conditions

Conditional statements allow you to execute different code based on different conditions.

### Conditional Statements Overview

| Statement | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `if` | Execute code if condition is true |
| `if...else` | Execute one block if true, another if false |
| `if...elseif...else` | Multiple conditions |
| `switch` | Select among many blocks |
| `match` (PHP 8) | Expression-based matching |
| Ternary | Shorthand if-else |

```php
<?php
$age = 18;

if ($age >= 18) {
    echo "Adult";
}
?>
```

### Comparison and Logical Operators in Conditions

| Operator | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| `==` | Equal |
| `===` | Identical |
| `!=` | Not equal |
| `>` / `<` | Greater / Less than |
| `&&` | AND |
| `||` | OR |
| `!` | NOT |

> **Key Point:** Always use `===` for comparisons when you need to match both value and type.
codeExample:
<?php
$score = 85;
$passed = true;

if ($score >= 75 && $passed) {
    echo "Congratulations, you passed with distinction!";
} elseif ($score >= 50) {
    echo "You passed.";
} else {
    echo "You failed. Try again.";
}
?>

quiz.question: Which statement type allows multiple condition branches?
